verses.text: Reading Jeremiah, Daniel understands the seventy years, and sets his face 'to seek by prayer and supplications, with fasting.' Scripture stirs prayer. God's promises move the godly to seek their fulfilment.

Reading Jeremiah, Daniel understands the seventy years, and sets his face 'to seek by prayer and supplications, with fasting.' Scripture stirs prayer. God's promises move the godly to seek their fulfilment.

Reading Jeremiah, Daniel understands the seventy years, and sets his face 'to seek by prayer and supplications, with fasting.' Scripture stirs prayer. God's promises move the godly to seek their fulfilment.
He confesses, 'O Lord, the great and dreadful God... we have sinned,' and have not heeded the prophets. Honest confession. True prayer owns the sin that brought the judgment.
He confesses, 'O Lord, the great and dreadful God... we have sinned,' and have not heeded the prophets. Honest confession. True prayer owns the sin that brought the judgment.
He confesses, 'O Lord, the great and dreadful God... we have sinned,' and have not heeded the prophets. Honest confession. True prayer owns the sin that brought the judgment.
'O Lord, righteousness belongeth unto thee, but unto us confusion of face'; yet to Him belong 'mercies and forgivenesses.' God just and merciful. The penitent flees from God's justice to His mercy.
'O Lord, righteousness belongeth unto thee, but unto us confusion of face'; yet to Him belong 'mercies and forgivenesses.' God just and merciful. The penitent flees from God's justice to His mercy.
'O Lord, righteousness belongeth unto thee, but unto us confusion of face'; yet to Him belong 'mercies and forgivenesses.' God just and merciful. The penitent flees from God's justice to His mercy.
Because they obeyed not, the curse is poured out; God has confirmed His word by this great evil. Word fulfilled. Even in judgment, God proves Himself faithful to His warnings.
Because they obeyed not, the curse is poured out; God has confirmed His word by this great evil. Word fulfilled. Even in judgment, God proves Himself faithful to His warnings.
Because they obeyed not, the curse is poured out; God has confirmed His word by this great evil. Word fulfilled. Even in judgment, God proves Himself faithful to His warnings.
All this evil is come, yet they made not their prayer to turn from iniquity; 'the LORD our God is righteous.' Slow to repent. Daniel justifies God and mourns the nation's hardness.
All this evil is come, yet they made not their prayer to turn from iniquity; 'the LORD our God is righteous.' Slow to repent. Daniel justifies God and mourns the nation's hardness.
All this evil is come, yet they made not their prayer to turn from iniquity; 'the LORD our God is righteous.' Slow to repent. Daniel justifies God and mourns the nation's hardness.
He pleads, turn Thine anger from Jerusalem; 'O Lord, hear; O Lord, forgive... defer not,' for Thy city bears Thy name. Earnest intercession. He grounds his plea on God's own honour.
He pleads, turn Thine anger from Jerusalem; 'O Lord, hear; O Lord, forgive... defer not,' for Thy city bears Thy name. Earnest intercession. He grounds his plea on God's own honour.
He pleads, turn Thine anger from Jerusalem; 'O Lord, hear; O Lord, forgive... defer not,' for Thy city bears Thy name. Earnest intercession. He grounds his plea on God's own honour.
He pleads, turn Thine anger from Jerusalem; 'O Lord, hear; O Lord, forgive... defer not,' for Thy city bears Thy name. Earnest intercession. He grounds his plea on God's own honour.
While he prays and confesses, Gabriel comes 'swiftly' and touches him at the evening oblation. Prayer answered. Heaven moves the moment the heart truly seeks God.
While he prays and confesses, Gabriel comes 'swiftly' and touches him at the evening oblation. Prayer answered. Heaven moves the moment the heart truly seeks God.
Gabriel says he is come to give him understanding, for 'at the beginning of thy supplications the commandment came forth.' Beloved and heard. God answers His praying servant without delay.
Gabriel says he is come to give him understanding, for 'at the beginning of thy supplications the commandment came forth.' Beloved and heard. God answers His praying servant without delay.
'Seventy weeks are determined upon thy people... to finish the transgression... and to bring in everlasting righteousness.' The great prophecy. All points to the work of Messiah.
From the commandment to 'Messiah the Prince' are sixty-nine weeks; then 'Messiah shall be cut off, but not for himself.' The cross foretold. Christ would die not for His own sin, but for ours.
From the commandment to 'Messiah the Prince' are sixty-nine weeks; then 'Messiah shall be cut off, but not for himself.' The cross foretold. Christ would die not for His own sin, but for ours.
He shall confirm a covenant with many and 'cause the sacrifice... to cease.' Sacrifice fulfilled. The death of Christ ends the need for all other offerings.
